head_drift_note: Contract specified starting_head=10f00a9 (v0.2.3); actual HEAD at start of work was 7e23fdb (v0.2.4). Re-anchored line numbers; observed +1 to +4 line drift in main.rs (DaemonCommand enum 382 -> 383; dispatch arms 509-514 -> 514-519). All other anchors stable.

tdd_workflow.red_root_cause: clap rejects 'daemon list' as unrecognized subcommand; migration test fails because 'daemon list' bails out before triggering migration

val006_daemon_startup_race_test.comment: start_rs_does_not_call_cleanup_stale_pid_before_lock — cleanup_stale_pid( text remains absent from start.rs

val013_daemon_status_cross_cwd_test.comment: no-arg `daemon status` from /tmp still finds the running daemon when only one is registered (status.rs dispatches to single entry)

public_types: DaemonRegistryEntry { project: PathBuf, pid: u32, socket: PathBuf, started_at: String }
registry_api_surface.concurrency_strategy: bounded compare-and-swap (3 attempts) via mtime-check + atomic write-tmp-then-rename; returns Err(io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock) on contention exhaustion

cli_surface_added.daemon_list: tldr daemon list (no flags; output controlled by global --format)
cli_surface_added.daemon_status_multi_daemon_dispatch: no-arg `daemon status`: 0 entries -> legacy fallback; 1 entry -> use it; 2+ entries -> error 'multiple daemons running ({n}); use --project <abs-path> or run "tldr daemon list"'

migration_shim.behavior: On first registry access (registry file absent), reads <cache>/tldr/daemon-active.json (or env-overridden equivalent); if PID alive, appends as registry entry; deletes legacy file unconditionally so it cannot block subsequent registry creation

files_modified: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/stop.rs (added --all flag and run_stop_all; remove_entry alongside remove_active in 3 sites)

guardrails.val006_structural: PASS — `cleanup_stale_pid(` text remains absent from start.rs (verified by grep + val006 structural test)
guardrails.val013_behavioral: PASS — no-arg daemon status from /tmp still resolves single live daemon
guardrails.no_new_production_dep: PASS — chrono is already a workspace dep (used for RFC3339 timestamps)
guardrails.no_fs2: PASS — option (c) bounded CAS retry only
guardrails.disjoint_scope: PASS — no taint.rs / embedder.rs / exhaustive_matrix.rs touches

open_followups: M5 v0.4.0 design doc may reference daemon_registry as an example of bounded-CAS pattern.
Future: if cross-process contention pressure ever exceeds 3 attempts on this code path, escalate to fs2 advisory lock (option a).
